{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",

  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Soft foot condition present and uncorrected",
    "Dial indicator reading indicates excessive base-to-foot gap exceeding API 686 tolerance",
    "Foundation or baseplate unevenness transmitting stress into machine casing"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop alignment work and correct soft foot by inserting precision shims under machine feet",
    "Re-measure soft foot with dial indicator until all feet show less than 0.002 in (0.05 mm) residual gap",
    "Perform final shaft alignment only after soft foot is eliminated"
  ],
  "rationale": "API 686 specifies that soft foot must be corrected before final shaft alignment; uncorrected soft foot preloads the machine frame, distorts alignment readings, and leads to premature bearing or coupling failure. The observed dial reading in the provided ME photo indicates a gap exceeding typical laser/dial alignment tolerances (commonly 0.002 in or 0.05 mm max), mandating a fail decision.",
  "references": [
    "API 686 Recommended Practice for Machinery Installation and Installation Design, Chapter 7 (Alignment)",
    "Laser system alignment manufacturer's guidance on soft foot correction limits"
  ]
}
